bindings.quest_action: scout the enemy camp and confirm they have sick children
bindings.quest_focus: the discovery that our enemies are desperate parents, not monsters
bindings.quest_stakes: if the settlement finds out I sympathize, I'll be cast out
bindings.quest_threat: a war being waged against families trying to keep their young alive
bindings.quest_origin: a scouting mission that revealed something nobody wanted to see

dialogueChunks.intro: I need to tell someone this, {player_name}, before it eats me alive. I've been carrying a secret that could get me cast out of {location}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: There's a {enemy_type} camp {location_hint}. Everyone here wants them dead. But during {quest_origin}, I saw {quest_focus}. They have sick children. They've been stealing our {quest_item} because they're trying to keep their young alive. I need someone to {quest_action}.
dialogueChunks.outro: If I'm right, then {quest_threat}. We've been wrong about them all along.

playerResponses.text: That changes things. I'll go see for myself.
Enemies are enemies. But I'll look, if only to prove you wrong.
What they are doesn't change what they've done. Count me out.

bindings.quest_action: bring supplies to the enemy's kin hiding in the wilderness
bindings.quest_focus: a wounded enemy who spoke of being driven from their homeland
bindings.quest_stakes: being labeled a traitor for showing compassion to the enemy
bindings.quest_threat: something far worse than them is driving these creatures from their home
bindings.quest_origin: a wounded creature that stumbled into town and was given mercy

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, do you believe that a person you've hated your whole life can turn out to be someone worth saving? Because I'm starting to.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_origin} three nights ago. {target_npc} insisted on patching it up. While it healed, it spoke to me: {quest_focus}. It said {quest_threat}, and they need {quest_item} to survive the journey. It begged me to {quest_action}, hiding {location_hint}.
dialogueChunks.outro: If anyone finds out I'm even considering this, {quest_stakes}. But I can't unhear what it said.

playerResponses.text: Compassion isn't treason. I'll bring the supplies.
This could be a trap. But I'll investigate.
Helping the enemy isn't something I'm willing to do.

bindings.quest_action: investigate whether these creatures are truly soulless monsters
bindings.quest_focus: a carved pendant of a child's face that stopped me from killing
bindings.quest_stakes: everything this settlement believes about them could be a lie
bindings.quest_threat: a cycle of violence fueled by assumptions nobody has questioned
bindings.quest_origin: a moment of mercy during a raid when a warrior showed me its child's pendant

dialogueChunks.intro: I've done something I can't take back, {player_name}. And I think I need help before it unravels.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: During the last raid on {location}, I cornered a {enemy_type} warrior. It dropped its weapon and showed me {quest_focus}. It said one word: 'please.' I let it go. Now {target_npc} is asking about the raider who escaped. I need someone to go {location_hint} and {quest_action}. I need {quest_item} as proof of peaceful contact.

playerResponses.text: The truth matters more than comfort. I'll find it.
You let one go? That was reckless. But fine, I'll look into it.
Some truths are better left buried. I won't be part of this.
